name: Water Stewardship Standards and Metrics
description: Reference framework for water stewardship covering the Alliance for Water Stewardship (AWS) Standard, CEO Water Mandate, water footprint methodology, and WRI Aqueduct risk indicators.

awsStandard.name: Alliance for Water Stewardship International Water Stewardship Standard
awsStandard.description: The AWS Standard provides a globally applicable framework for major water users to understand their water use and impacts, and to work collaboratively and transparently for sustainable water management within the catchment context.

waterRiskAssessmentMethodology.description: AWS defines a 5-step approach to water stewardship, progressing from understanding to continuous improvement.

steps.name: Gather and Understand
steps.description: Gather data to understand the site's water-related status, risks, and opportunities, and the shared water challenges in the catchment.
actions: Map the site's water balance (inputs, outputs, consumption, discharge)
Understand the catchment context: physical, regulatory, governance, and stakeholder landscape
Identify shared water challenges: scarcity, quality, ecosystem health, WASH access, governance
Assess water-related risks and opportunities for the site and catchment
Understand the site's indirect water use (supply chain and product use)

steps.name: Commit and Plan
steps.description: Commit to being a responsible water steward and develop a water stewardship plan that responds to shared water challenges.
actions: Make a public commitment to water stewardship
Develop a water stewardship plan with targets, actions, and timelines
Allocate resources and assign responsibilities
Plan for collective action with other stakeholders in the catchment

steps.name: Implement
steps.description: Implement the water stewardship plan, including internal water management improvements and collective action in the catchment.
actions: Implement water balance improvements (efficiency, reuse, alternative sources)
Maintain or improve water quality of discharges
Protect and restore Important Water-Related Areas (IWRAs)
Provide access to safe water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) for workers
Engage in collective action to address shared water challenges

steps.name: Evaluate
steps.description: Evaluate the site's water stewardship performance and the effectiveness of collective actions.
actions: Evaluate water stewardship performance against plan targets
Assess the effectiveness of collective actions
Review incident responses and emergency preparedness
Report on water stewardship performance annually

steps.name: Communicate and Disclose
steps.description: Communicate and disclose water stewardship efforts, performance, and ongoing challenges transparently.
actions: Disclose water stewardship activities, performance, and challenges publicly
Share knowledge and lessons with other water users and stakeholders
Contribute to collective governance of the catchment
Support public policy for sustainable water management

certificationLevels.description: Meets all core criteria of the AWS Standard
Meets core plus advanced criteria demonstrating leadership in water stewardship
Meets core and gold criteria plus demonstrates excellence, innovation, and advocacy in water stewardship

ceoWaterMandate.name: UN Global Compact CEO Water Mandate
ceoWaterMandate.description: The CEO Water Mandate is a UN Global Compact initiative that mobilises business leaders on water, sanitation, and the SDGs. It provides a platform for companies to commit to advancing water stewardship, WASH, and water-related SDG targets.

commitmentAreas.description: Measure and reduce water use, ensure wastewater quality, and increase water efficiency in own operations.
keyMetrics: Total water discharge by destination and quality
Water efficiency (water use per unit of production)

commitmentAreas.description: Engage with suppliers and stakeholders to improve water management across the value chain and in shared watersheds.
keyMetrics: Number of suppliers assessed for water risk

commitmentAreas.description: Advance water stewardship through partnerships, public-private collaboration, and support for effective water governance.
Support communities' access to safe water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH), particularly in water-stressed areas.
keyMetrics: Number of people reached with WASH programmes

commitmentAreas.description: Support good water governance and contribute to public policy dialogue on water management.
keyMetrics: Participation in multi-stakeholder water governance platforms

commitmentAreas.description: Report annually on water stewardship activities and progress, including through CDP Water, GRI, and other frameworks.

waterFootprint.name: Water Footprint Assessment (ISO 14046 / Water Footprint Network)
waterFootprint.description: Water footprint assessment quantifies the total volume of freshwater used to produce goods and services along the supply chain. It distinguishes between blue, green, and grey water footprints.

components.name: Blue Water Footprint
components.description: Volume of surface water and groundwater consumed (evaporated, incorporated into product, or transferred to another catchment) in the production of a good or service.
components.significance: Directly reduces available water for other users and ecosystems. Most relevant in water-stressed regions.
components.calculationNote: Blue water footprint = water withdrawn - water returned to the same catchment at the same quality

components.name: Green Water Footprint
components.description: Volume of rainwater consumed during the production process, particularly relevant to agriculture and forestry. This is rainwater that is evapotranspired or incorporated into the product.
sources: Rainwater stored in the root zone of the soil (soil moisture)
components.significance: Represents the use of soil moisture from rainfall. Most relevant for agricultural products. Generally considered less impactful than blue water use.
components.calculationNote: Green water footprint = total crop evapotranspiration - effective precipitation that would have been evapotranspired regardless

components.name: Grey Water Footprint
components.description: Volume of freshwater required to assimilate the load of pollutants in discharge water to meet ambient water quality standards. It represents the pollution dimension of water use.
sources: Not a physical water use but a theoretical volume representing pollution assimilation capacity
components.significance: Indicates the water pollution intensity of production. A large grey water footprint signals significant water quality impacts.
components.calculationNote: Grey water footprint = pollutant load / (maximum acceptable concentration - natural background concentration)

standards.name: ISO 14046:2014
standards.description: International standard for water footprint assessment, principles, requirements, and guidelines. Focuses on environmental impacts.

standards.name: Water Footprint Network Manual
standards.description: Water Footprint Assessment Manual (Hoekstra et al., 2011) — comprehensive methodology for blue, green, and grey water footprint calculation.

watershedMetrics.description: Key metrics for assessing water risk and stress at the watershed level.

metrics.name: Water Stress Index
metrics.description: Ratio of total annual water withdrawals to total available annual renewable water supply in a catchment. Higher values indicate greater competition for water resources.
metrics.formula: WSI = Total water withdrawals / Total renewable water supply

metrics.name: Baseline Water Stress
metrics.description: Ratio of total water withdrawals to total available renewable surface and groundwater supply, accounting for upstream consumptive use. The primary indicator in WRI Aqueduct.
metrics.formula: BWS = Total withdrawals / (Mean available blue water)

metrics.name: Water Depletion
metrics.description: Ratio of total water consumption to total available renewable water supply. Unlike water stress (which uses withdrawals), depletion uses consumption (the portion of withdrawn water not returned to the basin).
metrics.formula: WD = Total water consumption / Total renewable water supply

wriAqueductIndicators.name: WRI Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas
wriAqueductIndicators.description: The World Resources Institute (WRI) Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas maps and analyses current and future water risks globally. Aqueduct 4.0 uses the latest hydrological models and socioeconomic data.

indicators.name: Baseline Water Stress
indicators.description: Ratio of total water withdrawals to available renewable supply

indicators.name: Baseline Water Depletion
indicators.description: Ratio of total water consumption to available renewable supply

indicators.name: Interannual Variability
indicators.description: Year-to-year variation in available water supply

indicators.name: Seasonal Variability
indicators.description: Within-year variation in available water supply

indicators.name: Groundwater Table Decline
indicators.description: Rate of decline of groundwater levels

indicators.name: Drought Risk
indicators.description: Probability and severity of drought events

indicators.name: Riverine Flood Risk
indicators.description: Probability and severity of riverine flooding

indicators.name: Coastal Flood Risk
indicators.description: Probability and severity of coastal flooding from storm surge and sea-level rise

indicators.name: Untreated Connected Wastewater
indicators.description: Percentage of domestic wastewater that is connected to a treatment facility but not treated to at least secondary level

indicators.name: Coastal Eutrophication Potential
indicators.description: Potential for riverine nitrogen and phosphorus loading to stimulate algal blooms in coastal waters

indicators.name: Unimproved/No Drinking Water
indicators.description: Percentage of population without access to improved drinking water source

indicators.name: Unimproved/No Sanitation
indicators.description: Percentage of population without access to improved sanitation

indicators.name: Peak RepRisk ESG country score
indicators.description: Country-level ESG risk exposure score as it relates to water issues

overallWaterRiskScore.description: Composite score combining all indicators with category-specific weights. Scored 0-5.

futureProjections.description: Aqueduct 4.0 provides future projections of water stress and supply under different climate and socioeconomic scenarios.

sasbWaterMetricsBySector.description: SASB (now part of IFRS Foundation / ISSB) identifies sector-specific water-related disclosure topics and metrics for financially material water issues.

waterMetrics.name: Total fresh water withdrawn
waterMetrics.description: Total volume of fresh water withdrawn, with percentage in water-stressed regions

waterMetrics.name: Volume of produced water and flowback generated
waterMetrics.description: Volume, with percentage discharged, injected, recycled; hydrocarbon content in discharged water

waterMetrics.name: Percentage of hydraulically fractured wells with full chemical disclosure
Total water withdrawn
waterMetrics.description: With percentage from water-stressed regions, by fuel type

waterMetrics.name: Number of incidents of non-compliance with water permits, standards, regulations
Description of water management risks and strategies
waterMetrics.description: Discussion of risks and strategy, including cooling water and once-through vs closed-loop

waterMetrics.name: Description of water management risks and discussion of strategies
waterMetrics.description: Including irrigation practices, water efficiency, and sourcing from water-stressed regions

waterMetrics.name: Total fresh water withdrawn
waterMetrics.description: With percentage in water-stressed regions and percentage recycled

waterMetrics.name: Number of incidents of non-compliance with water permits
Water withdrawal data coverage
waterMetrics.description: Percentage of floor area for which water withdrawal data is available

waterMetrics.name: Total water withdrawn by portfolio area with/without water stress
waterMetrics.description: Disaggregated by water-stressed and non-water-stressed regions

waterMetrics.name: Like-for-like change in water withdrawn for portfolio area
Description of water management risks and strategies
waterMetrics.description: Including efficiency measures and water recycling

waterMetrics.name: Total water withdrawn
waterMetrics.description: With percentage in water-stressed regions and percentage recycled

waterMetrics.name: Number of incidents of non-compliance with water permits
waterMetrics.description: Violations of discharge permits or water quality standards

waterMetrics.name: Description of water management risks
waterMetrics.description: Including process water use, cooling water, and chemical discharge management

waterMetrics.name: Total water withdrawn
waterMetrics.description: With percentage in water-stressed regions and percentage recycled. Semiconductor manufacturing is water-intensive (ultrapure water for wafer fabrication).
